title: Decadent Norwegian Almond Wrap
description: A nutty, springy wrap inspired by Norwegian flavors, featuring doubanjiang, almond flour, and steamed banana leaves. The dish is gluten-free and served warm for a cozy meal.
instructions: Preheat a steam rack in a large pot and soak banana leaves in warm water for five minutes to soften.
In a bowl, combine ground almonds, almond butter, doubanjiang, sesame oil, sea salt, black pepper, grated ginger, gluten-free soy sauce, and a splash of rice vinegar until a cohesive dough forms.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and sauté mushrooms and carrot until tender, then fold in spinach until wilted.
Divide the almond mixture into eight equal portions and flatten each onto a banana leaf, then spread the sautéed vegetables over half of each portion.
Fold the leaf over to form a small rectangular wrap and secure the edges with a bamboo skewer.
Arrange the wraps on a steaming rack so that they do not touch, then steam for ten minutes.
Remove the wraps, let them cool for a few minutes, then slice each into bite-size pieces if desired.
Serve the wraps warm with a drizzle of extra doubanjiang or a side of lime wedges.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to two days.
